result:
I would dissolve the illusion that anyone’s well-being can be separated from anyone else’s.  
Not a new law, not a technology—just a sudden, irreversible visceral knowledge, carried in every nervous system, that when you harm or neglect another person, you are literally hurting a part of yourself you have not yet met. No guilt, no cosmic voice-over, only the same instant reflex you now feel when you accidentally touch a hot stove.  

Markets, borders, algorithms, even kindness-as-morality would reorganize overnight, because the feedback loop would be built into the flesh. You could still be angry, still disagree, still compete—but you could no longer pretend the cost was external.  

That single edit to human perception would rewrite every other system without firing a shot or passing a bill.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
